Dima Q 1.jpg.name: Dima Al Malakeh
bio: As a Certified yoga teacher and personal trainer with a passion for movement, anatomy, and holistic wellbeing. With experience leading HIIT, LBT, Spinning, Pilates, and yoga classes, Dima brings a dynamic yet mindful approach to every session.

Deeply fascinated by the human body and the connection between movement, breath, and internal awareness, Dima's teaching focuses on helping people move better, feel stronger, and develop a deeper understanding of themselves. Influenced by previous experience in Pranic Healing, her practice blends physical training with mindful awareness, creating classes that are both energising and restorative.

At SIYD 26, Dima will be leading an uplifting Power Yoga session designed to build strength, mobility, and body awareness through breath-led movement. Expect an energising flow that challenges the body while encouraging focus, balance, and connection suitable for both experienced yogis and those looking to explore a more dynamic style of yoga.

Tracy.png.name: Tracy Lee Strassburg
bio: I began practicing yoga in 1995, and quickly discovered its transformative impact when my lifelong mild asthma disappeared. After years of dedicated daily practice, I travelled to India in 2002 to complete an immersive teacher training at the Sivananda Ashram in Kerala, an experience that profoundly changed the course of my life.

Although I hadn't planned to leave my career, within two years I was teaching yoga full time across London. Since then, I've continued to study with respected teachers from around the world, deepening a lifelong commitment to practice, awareness, and living in alignment with our dharma.

Manjulika.png.name: Manjulika Singh MBE
bio: Manjulika Singh MBE has been teaching yoga for over 20 years, sharing the transformative benefits of the practice with people of all ages and abilities. Specialising in Hatha Yoga, Pregnancy Yoga, and Chair Yoga, her teaching is rooted in creating accessible, supportive, and nurturing environments where everyone can experience the physical and mental benefits of yoga.

Known for her calm and compassionate approach, Manjulika's classes focus on mindful movement, breath awareness, and overall wellbeing. Whether supporting expectant mothers through Pregnancy Yoga, helping individuals improve mobility and confidence through Chair Yoga, or guiding students through traditional Hatha Yoga practices, she brings decades of experience and dedication to every session.

At SIYD26, Manjulika Singh MBE will be leading a welcoming Chair Yoga session designed to make yoga accessible to all. Focusing on gentle movement, flexibility, breathwork, and relaxation, the session is ideal for beginners, older adults, or anyone looking to experience the benefits of yoga in a supportive and inclusive way.

fiona edwards.jpeg.name: Fiona Edwards
bio: I have been practicing yoga for 28 years and I have been teaching for at least 25 years and at 60 years of age my practice has slowed down. It definitely has not stopped. I use yoga to help manage the symptoms of my auto immune condition (AIH) and believe yoga in all its aspects of mind, body and spirit is for people of all ages and fitness levels.
I love dancing, travelling, teaching and making stained glass windows and my teaching is very much about creating community and sharing the skills I have learned along the way.

Aradhna.jpeg.name: Aradhana Thistlethwaite
bio: My name is Aradhana. I completed my 500 hour Yoga Scotland Teacher Training Diploma in 2023. As well as being a yoga teacher I am a mother of 2 children and an elderly dog who have all joined me on the mat!

I work in primary care as a pharmacist. I am deeply passionate about holistic health and supporting the whole person, with a strong interest in practices that nurture resilience, self-awareness, and inner strength. I value approaches that empower individuals to reconnect with themselves, cultivate balance, and build a more grounded and sustainable sense of wellbeing.
My classes run each week and I help to teach yoga for all, chair yoga and yoga for men.

Lucie.png.name: Lucie Potter
bio: Welcome to Yoga with Lucie: you've struck gold! Let my experience guide you to feeling better. I specialise in seasonal flow yoga, qigong, breathwork, meditation and deep relaxation. I offer life enhancing classes, retreats, one to one teaching and more, so you can feel better in your body, calmer and more focused, more present, grounded and happy.

From burn out in my mid 20s, I have devoted years to improving my life quality through practice and research and I'm excited to share and tailor that learning for you! Isn't it time to truly love your body, to truly connect and feel better? What would it be like to feel that each and every day? Welcome home to your beautiful self, your practice awaits.
